retrieval_candidates.natural_language_intent: Create a magazine page layout with a bold headline “Then vs. Now” in large, light-colored sans-serif font at the top center, positioned above two columns of text. At the top left, add a small “ADVERTISEMENT” label in black sans-serif font. content.textBody:
still know each other. Schools are still top-tier. Community events, local businesses, and strong city services make it a place people are proud to call home.

Why Kobs & Martin
At Kobs & Martin, we’re more than just realtors—we’re your local guides. With deep roots in Richardson and years of area experience, we help clients navigate where the city has been and where it’s going. Whether you’re buying or selling, we bring the knowledge, strategy, and care you need.

Richardson has grown, but its soul is unchanged—and we get to live in the “Best Of” it.

exceptional schools, plus added amenities and lifestyle perks. Developments like CityLine and the CORE District have brought new restaurants, retail, entertainment, and walkable living. Public transportation has expanded, and the city continues to invest in parks, trails, and infrastructure.

Modern buyers are drawn to a mix of beautifully maintained mid-century homes and new construction options. Median home prices now range from $375,000 to over $550,000 in some neighborhoods and from $750,000 to $1,200,000 and over in others. Most new builds in Richardson start at $1,800,000 and go up from there—proof of strong appreciation and long-term value. Whether it’s first-time buyers, growing families, or downsizing empty nesters, Richardson real estate offers something for everyone.

What Hasn’t Changed
Despite the growth, the heart of Richardson remains the same. Neighbors

Then vs. Now
How Richardson Real Estate 
Became the Best

When people first moved to Richardson decades ago, it was all about family-friendly neighborhoods, top-rated schools, and a quieter pace of life just outside the Dallas hustle. As local realtors, we’ve watched the city evolve without losing the qualities that made it special in the first place.

Then: A Suburban Gem
In the 1970s and ’80s, Richardson was a hidden gem—safe, suburban, and community-oriented. Young families were drawn in by strong RISD schools, affordable homes, and the promise of a quality life. Homes ranged from $40,000 to $80,000, with established neighborhoods like Canyon Creek and Greenwood Hills offering charm and space. The nearby Telecom Corridor made Richardson an ideal spot for professionals working in tech and engineering.
Now: A Connected, Modern Community
Today, Richardson is no longer a secret. It’s a thriving, connected city with the same

content.textBody:
How did you come up with the name Chérie Lane and how is the name pronounced?
The name came from my sorority’s little sister, meaning “sweetheart” in French. It’s pronounced “shuh-REE lane.”
 Why II Creeks?
We chose II Creeks because it truly reflects the heart of our brand. Richardson is a vibrant, growing community filled with
collections and luxe denim to timeless seasonal capsules, Chérie Lane is more than just a boutique; it’s a community where fashion and empowerment go hand in hand.

CHÉRIE LANE
A brand new boutique in Canyon Creek!

Tell us about yourself and Chérie Lane.
 Hi! My name is Abby Lane and I am the owner of Chérie Lane, a fashion-forward boutique built on the belief that every woman deserves to feel confident, beautiful and unforgettable in every setting. Our signature style is classy, chic, and sparkly—curated for tenacious and driven women who love to make a statement. From bold game day

Hi my name is Abby Lane and I am the owner of Chérie Lane. Chérie Lane is a fashion-forward boutique built on the belief that every woman deserves to feel confident, beautiful, and unforgettable in every setting she walks into. Founded in 2022 as a college project at Baylor University, Chérie Lane has grown into a thriving e-commerce brand \nd now a flagship storefront in Richardson, Texas.

content.textBody:
The word BEST, by definition, means: that which is the most excellent, outstanding, or desirable. 

RICHARDSON: a city in Texas boasting a population of close to 120,000 people, is the city we call home. 

BEST OF RICHARDSON: a contest compromised of businesses, organizations, people, events and locations throughout the city that is voted on by readers and members of the community, representing all that makes Richardson the best place to call home.

What’s exciting about the Best of Richardson awards is that being named the Best of Richardson in any category is a prestigious honor. If you followed along this voting journey, you know it’s been a methodical process of gathering all the names of the beloved people, places and businesses around Richardson during the April nomination phase. In this first round, the readers of Richardson Life Magazine showed up with more than 950 nominations in 200+ categories—amazing! 

Then, as the contest narrowed, the top three nominees with the most nominations in each category advanced to the finalist voting round in June. That round, you cast more than 50,000 votes to get us to today’s announcement of the winners by category. The moment so many have been waiting for. The BEST of Richardson. 

Thank you, Richardson Life Magazine readers, for showing up to support your community. And thank you to all the businesses and individuals for taking part and rallying your supporters. There are so many unbelievable places here to see and experience. I hope you’ll also check out our online directory, which includes not only the winners in each category, but the finalists as well. 

We’ll be back next year with another edition of Best of Richardson. If you don’t see one of your favorites listed, you can always nominate them next year to bring them to the winner’s circle.

We believe Richardson is the absolute BEST place to live.

retrieval_candidates.natural_language_intent: Create a layout for a “Student Athlete of the Month” feature with a bold, centered title at top left. Below, place two columns of text: left column for personal info (name, school, grade, hobbies), right column for brag section (honors, awards, academic info). Position a small italic photo credit below the main title. At bottom right, add a thin horizontal line with website URL. All gray rectangles are images; focus only on their position and size relative to text blocks.

content.textBody:
Brag on yourself: tell us your honors and awards in and out of school. 
I am going into my 3rd season starting on the varsity football team at Pearce and my 2nd year as a captain. I have been awarded all-district recognition the past two years and was awarded sophomore player of the year my first year as a varsity player. I am an A and B student in school and have always prided myself on working extremely hard at everything I do.

Tell us your name, school you’re attending, and grade. 
My name is Will Miltenberger and I am a senior at Pearce High School.

Tell us about yourself. 
My hobbies are playing sports and spending time with friends and family. I like to spend my time out of school watching college football (when in season), spending time outside, hunting, fishing, and being active in any way.

National Night Out is celebrating its 36th year in Richardson! The City of Richardson and the Canyon Creek Homeowners Association invite residents to participate in block parties or similar gatherings on Tuesday, October 7, 2025, from 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm. These events aim to increase connections among neighbors and strengthen police-community relations by engaging with First Responders. As RPD Chief Gary Tittle stated in Richardson’s 2025 National Night Out preview video, "We are strongest when we stand together."
No official theme has been designated for this year’s National Night Out, but residents of Canyon Creek are encouraged to display blue bows on trees as a demonstration of support.

content.textBody:
Happy August! If you’ve been a regular reader of Richardson Life Magazine, you’ll notice that this issue looks a little—actually, a lot—different than the usual. Some of our normally featured articles are absent and the calendar of events and Scene section are missing. They’ll be back in future issues. Instead, the pages of this Best of Richardson issue are dedicated to the companies, individuals and organizations you all voted as 2025 stand-outs. 

In spite of the temporary changes, you’ll still get to hear from our new mayor, Amir Omar, about his first ninety days in office. And you’ll see how RISD kicked off the school year by celebrating its teachers and offering hands-on training to prepare them for the school year. They truly are the BEST school district, thanks to Superintendent Tabitha Branum’s leadership, her administrative team and all the teachers, counselors, principals, culinary team members, support staff and others who make RISD run. In spite of recent budget cuts, Branum and her team continue to deliver a top-notch education to Richardson’s students.

And although we may not have a calendar in this issue, I’d like to point out a couple of important upcoming events. On August 28 at 7:00 pm, the 15th annual Richardson Real Heroes Awards Celebration, sponsored by the City of Richardson, will take place at the Eisemann Center’s Bank of America Theatre. 2025 award recipients Dr. Mona Alnaeemi and Neil Stampe will be honored for their outstanding volunteer service to the Richardson community, along with the 2025 nominees. The awards ceremony’s special guest will be Texas-based singer/songwriter/speaker Sara Hickman, who will weave song into her talk about the power of giving back.  Tickets are free but must be obtained from the Eisemann Center Box Office. Details can be found at the RRH website at richardsonrealheroes.com/awardsceremony.  

Additionally, Network is hosting its 28th Annual Golf Outing on Friday, September 12, at Sherrill Park. If you’re a golfer and want to support an amazing cause, sign up today at thenetwork.org/golf. Network continues to meet the needs of our community in large ways and small and needs our support now more than ever. If you’re not a golfer, visit Network’s website to see how your gifts and talents align with their mission.

Aren’t we lucky to live in Richardson? I know I am!

Below it, a larger gray rectangle (image) centered beneath the text block. The text should flow naturally with the images, using specified fonts and sizes. All gray rectangles are placeholders for images; focus only on their placement and size relative to text.

content.textBody: Owen enjoying a day out and about
Olivia in her photo op installation at the 2024 Wildflower! Arts & Music festival

Work and Community
By day, Mike is VP of Marketing for a Romanian FinTech company, while Julie manages an interior design office in Dallas. Beyond their careers, both are deeply woven into the Richardson arts scene. They serve on the Executive Steering Committee for the Wildflower! Arts and Music Festival, helping produce the Budding Talent Vocal Competition each year. Julie also lends her talents to the Richardson Symphony Orchestra’s Beaujolais Bash fundraiser, while Mike dedicated years as Scoutmaster for Troop 895. Together, they host block parties, organize National Night Out, and jump at any chance to build community through music and connection.

Passions and Pastimes
Outside of work and volunteering, Mike can often be found at White Rock Lake, sailing with the Corinthian Sailing Club. While his official title is board member,

home with them after two rehomings, may be eight years old but still acts like a big baby. Then there’s Harry Styles—the hound dog, not the pop star—who joined the family as a puppy and has been Bennie’s partner-in-crime ever since. But make no mistake: the real boss is Lupe, their sociable 10-year-old cat who keeps everyone in line.

Family Time, Then and Now
With the kids grown, family time looks a little different these days, but traditions remain strong. Whether it’s storytelling around a campfire, a lively game night, or planning trips centered on music festivals, the Hughes family always finds a way to connect. One of their favorite adventures was road-tripping to Montana for the Decemberists’ Traveling Rest Festival, with a detour to Yellowstone.

Top-left of the grid has "Family of the Month" (4) and top-right has "Business of the Month" (8), both with large numbers above. content.textBody:
has a married son and my granddaughter will soon be 24. I’m proud of my family for sure. I worked for Safeco Insurance Company for 30 years before retiring. My hubby, Bob, passed away in 2017. We would have been married 61 years this year.

What are your involvements either in Canyon Creek, through your church, or within the community?
I’m a member of the Richardson Woman’s Club and have held several different positions over the years. I’m also a member of First United Methodist Church Richardson and play Bridge in several area groups.

What do you love most about living in Canyon Creek? 
What I love about living in Canyon Creek is the people. We are all like family on our block. If you are in need, just let a neighbor know and your prayers will be

the streets of Dallas when Doodle Dandy picked him up and found a home for him. He’s my buddy and is settled into my routine. I’m so grateful to have him as he’s a wonderful companion. We’ve been together 6 years and I don’t know what I would do without him.

Tell us about your family. How long have you lived in Canyon Creek?
I’ve lived in Canyon Creek for 48 years and that is a record for me. When I was growing up, we would move every 3 years. I went to 3 elementary schools, 3 high schools, and 3 colleges. I have two grown daughters who live in the area. Lynn lives in Rockwall and is the CFO of Chiloso Bistro Restaurants. She has 3 boys who have all graduated from college and are working in the area.   
Kristen lives in Allen and is an executive assistant to the HR officer of a large worldwide construction company. She

Tell us your name and the name of your pet(s). What type of pet is he/she?
I’m Georgia Nelson and my dog’s name is Murphy. He is a border collie/poodle (bordoodle).

How did Murphy become a member of your family? 
I used to dog sit for people in the neighborhood who had gotten him as a rescue from Doodle Dandy. One time I sat for two weeks with Murphy while his owner was in Seattle. When she came home, she told me she was going to return Murphy to Doodle Dandy. I asked her not to because Murphy and I had bonded and we were friends, so she offered him to me. Naturally I stepped up and took him!

Tell us more about Murphy.
I’ve had dogs all my life, but never this lovable and sweet. Murphy just wants to be loved. We found out he was roaming

content.textBody:
still know each other. Schools are still top-tier. Community events, local businesses, and strong city services make it a place people are proud to call home.

Why Kobs & Martin
At Kobs & Martin, we’re more than just realtors—we’re your local guides. With deep roots in Richardson and years of area experience, we help clients navigate where the city has been and where it’s going. Whether you’re buying or selling, we bring the knowledge, strategy, and care you need.

Richardson has grown, but its soul is unchanged—and we get to live in the “Best Of” it.

exceptional schools, plus added amenities and lifestyle perks. Developments like CityLine and the CORE District have brought new restaurants, retail, entertainment, and walkable living. Public transportation has expanded, and the city continues to invest in parks, trails, and infrastructure.

Modern buyers are drawn to a mix of beautifully maintained mid-century homes and new construction options. Median home prices now range from $375,000 to over $550,000 in some neighborhoods and from $750,000 to $1,200,000 and over in others. Most new builds in Richardson start at $1,800,000 and go up from there—proof of strong appreciation and long-term value. Whether it’s first-time buyers, growing families, or downsizing empty nesters, Richardson real estate offers something for everyone.

What Hasn’t Changed
Despite the growth, the heart of Richardson remains the same. Neighbors

Then vs. Now
How Richardson Real Estate 
Became the Best

When people first moved to Richardson decades ago, it was all about family-friendly neighborhoods, top-rated schools, and a quieter pace of life just outside the Dallas hustle. As local realtors, we’ve watched the city evolve without losing the qualities that made it special in the first place.

Then: A Suburban Gem
In the 1970s and ’80s, Richardson was a hidden gem—safe, suburban, and community-oriented. Young families were drawn in by strong RISD schools, affordable homes, and the promise of a quality life. Homes ranged from $40,000 to $80,000, with established neighborhoods like Canyon Creek and Greenwood Hills offering charm and space. The nearby Telecom Corridor made Richardson an ideal spot for professionals working in tech and engineering.
Now: A Connected, Modern Community
Today, Richardson is no longer a secret. It’s a thriving, connected city with the same

content.textBody: Owen enjoying a day out and about
Olivia in her photo op installation at the 2024 Wildflower! Arts & Music festival

Work and Community
By day, Mike is VP of Marketing for a Romanian FinTech company, while Julie manages an interior design office in Dallas. Beyond their careers, both are deeply woven into the Richardson arts scene. They serve on the Executive Steering Committee for the Wildflower! Arts and Music Festival, helping produce the Budding Talent Vocal Competition each year. Julie also lends her talents to the Richardson Symphony Orchestra’s Beaujolais Bash fundraiser, while Mike dedicated years as Scoutmaster for Troop 895. Together, they host block parties, organize National Night Out, and jump at any chance to build community through music and connection.

Passions and Pastimes
Outside of work and volunteering, Mike can often be found at White Rock Lake, sailing with the Corinthian Sailing Club. While his official title is board member,

home with them after two rehomings, may be eight years old but still acts like a big baby. Then there’s Harry Styles—the hound dog, not the pop star—who joined the family as a puppy and has been Bennie’s partner-in-crime ever since. But make no mistake: the real boss is Lupe, their sociable 10-year-old cat who keeps everyone in line.

Family Time, Then and Now
With the kids grown, family time looks a little different these days, but traditions remain strong. Whether it’s storytelling around a campfire, a lively game night, or planning trips centered on music festivals, the Hughes family always finds a way to connect. One of their favorite adventures was road-tripping to Montana for the Decemberists’ Traveling Rest Festival, with a detour to Yellowstone.

content.textBody:
Happy August! If you’ve been a regular reader of Richardson Life Magazine, you’ll notice that this issue looks a little—actually, a lot—different than the usual. Some of our normally featured articles are absent and the calendar of events and Scene section are missing. They’ll be back in future issues. Instead, the pages of this Best of Richardson issue are dedicated to the companies, individuals and organizations you all voted as 2025 stand-outs. 

In spite of the temporary changes, you’ll still get to hear from our new mayor, Amir Omar, about his first ninety days in office. And you’ll see how RISD kicked off the school year by celebrating its teachers and offering hands-on training to prepare them for the school year. They truly are the BEST school district, thanks to Superintendent Tabitha Branum’s leadership, her administrative team and all the teachers, counselors, principals, culinary team members, support staff and others who make RISD run. In spite of recent budget cuts, Branum and her team continue to deliver a top-notch education to Richardson’s students.

And although we may not have a calendar in this issue, I’d like to point out a couple of important upcoming events. On August 28 at 7:00 pm, the 15th annual Richardson Real Heroes Awards Celebration, sponsored by the City of Richardson, will take place at the Eisemann Center’s Bank of America Theatre. 2025 award recipients Dr. Mona Alnaeemi and Neil Stampe will be honored for their outstanding volunteer service to the Richardson community, along with the 2025 nominees. The awards ceremony’s special guest will be Texas-based singer/songwriter/speaker Sara Hickman, who will weave song into her talk about the power of giving back.  Tickets are free but must be obtained from the Eisemann Center Box Office. Details can be found at the RRH website at richardsonrealheroes.com/awardsceremony.  

Additionally, Network is hosting its 28th Annual Golf Outing on Friday, September 12, at Sherrill Park. If you’re a golfer and want to support an amazing cause, sign up today at thenetwork.org/golf. Network continues to meet the needs of our community in large ways and small and needs our support now more than ever. If you’re not a golfer, visit Network’s website to see how your gifts and talents align with their mission.

Aren’t we lucky to live in Richardson? I know I am!

content.textBody:
How did you come up with the name Chérie Lane and how is the name pronounced?
The name came from my sorority’s little sister, meaning “sweetheart” in French. It’s pronounced “shuh-REE lane.”
 Why II Creeks?
We chose II Creeks because it truly reflects the heart of our brand. Richardson is a vibrant, growing community filled with
collections and luxe denim to timeless seasonal capsules, Chérie Lane is more than just a boutique; it’s a community where fashion and empowerment go hand in hand.

CHÉRIE LANE
A brand new boutique in Canyon Creek!

Tell us about yourself and Chérie Lane.
 Hi! My name is Abby Lane and I am the owner of Chérie Lane, a fashion-forward boutique built on the belief that every woman deserves to feel confident, beautiful and unforgettable in every setting. Our signature style is classy, chic, and sparkly—curated for tenacious and driven women who love to make a statement. From bold game day

Hi my name is Abby Lane and I am the owner of Chérie Lane. Chérie Lane is a fashion-forward boutique built on the belief that every woman deserves to feel confident, beautiful, and unforgettable in every setting she walks into. Founded in 2022 as a college project at Baylor University, Chérie Lane has grown into a thriving e-commerce brand \nd now a flagship storefront in Richardson, Texas.

content.textBody:
are interested in becoming a Canyon Creek Block Captain and hosting a party, please contact Sharon Odell at sharon@the-odells.com. 
Block Captains have until September 19, 2025, to register their National Night Out events at richardsonpolice.net/services/community-engagement/national-night-out/nno-block-party-registration. For National Night Out 2025 questions, contact RPD Officer David Beregszaszy at (972) 744-4938.
As always, residents are encouraged to donate canned goods to the Network of Community Ministries’ food pantry during neighborhood events. Collected canned foods will be picked up by Richardson police officers, firefighters, paramedics and city government officials who will visit registered gatherings.
Let’s celebrate this special evening that ties our Canyon Creek community together on Tuesday, October 7th by visiting with

National Night Out is celebrating its 36th year in Richardson! The City of Richardson and the Canyon Creek Homeowners Association invite residents to participate in block parties or similar gatherings on Tuesday, October 7, 2025, from 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm. These events aim to increase connections among neighbors and strengthen police-community relations by engaging with First Responders. As RPD Chief Gary Tittle stated in Richardson’s 2025 National Night Out preview video, "We are strongest when we stand together."
No official theme has been designated for this year’s National Night Out, but residents of Canyon Creek are encouraged to display blue bows on trees as a demonstration of support. 
Canyon Creek is proud to host more block parties in Richardson than any other area. In 2024, for instance, 38 out of the city's 114 registered block parties took place in Canyon Creek. If you
